newsprint-cream.$description: Newsprint Cream — Page canvas, hero background, section backgrounds — the warm off-white that makes the entire interface feel printed rather than digital

paper-white.$description: Paper White — Card surfaces, elevated panels, form inputs, and any surface that needs to lift off the cream canvas

ink.$description: Ink — Primary text, body copy, links, heading text — slightly warmer than pure black, sits comfortably on cream

body-gray.$description: Body Gray — Secondary text and default body color where slightly lighter than Ink is needed without losing contrast

button-black.$description: Button Black — Supporting neutral for secondary UI, dividers, and muted labels. Do not promote it to the primary CTA color

marginalia-gray.$description: Marginalia Gray — Supporting neutral for secondary UI, dividers, and muted labels.

medium-content-sans-serif-font.$description: medium-content-sans-serif-font — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

gt-super.$description: Display headlines and hero — a transitional book serif at extreme scale with tight tracking and 0.83 line-height. The choice to use weight 400 (not bold) at 120px is the signature: the letterforms do the work, not the weight. The negative tracking pulls the optical gaps closed, making 'Human stories & ideas' read as a single typographic block.

shne.$description: UI sans-serif for navigation, buttons, subheadings, and small headings. The 13–14px range handles nav and button labels; 20–22px covers subheadings and secondary hero text. Clean grotesque with humanist proportions — readable at small sizes, unobtrusive at large.

charter-georgia-stack.$description: Body content serif for reading view — the long-form article font. Set at 16px with tight 1.2 line-height, optimized for the column width of Medium's reader.

shne-extended.$description: Body sans-serif for non-reading contexts — descriptions, cards, inline content. Matches the content serif's metrics so the two can coexist at the same size.

sohne.$description: sohne — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

times.$description: Times — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

canvas.$description: Surface level 1: Page-level background, hero, section bands — the warm paper base

paper.$description: Surface level 2: Cards, modals, inputs — surfaces that need to read as distinct objects on the cream canvas

ink-surface.$description: Surface level 3: Filled buttons and the wordmark — the only dark surface, used sparingly as visual anchor
